import scanpy as sc
import squidpy as sq
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import numpy as np
from scipy.ndimage import gaussian_filter

In [12]:
adata = sc.read_h5ad("mouse_brain_with_celltypes_s1r3.h5ad")
In [13]:
adata.obs["x"] = (adata.obs["min_x"] + adata.obs["max_x"]) / 2
adata.obs["y"] = (adata.obs["min_y"] + adata.obs["max_y"]) / 2

adata.obsm["spatial"] = np.column_stack([
    adata.obs["x"],
    adata.obs["y"]
])

adata.obsm.keys()
Out[13]:
KeysView(AxisArrays with keys: X_pca, X_umap, blank_genes, spatial)
In [14]:
# Convert all specified Cell Type labels to "Undefined"

to_undefined = [
    "Mesoderm: Cardiac",
    "Mesenchyme: Undefined",
    "Immune: Undefined",
    "Ectoderm: Early ectoderm",
    "Blood: Undefined",
    "Ectoderm: Lateral nasal pit",
    "Gastrulation: Nascent mesoderm",
    "Fibroblast: Early fibroblasts",
]

adata.obs["Cell Type"] = adata.obs["Cell Type"].replace(
    to_undefined,
    "Undefined"
)

/tmp/ipykernel_19105/3390368061.py:14: FutureWarning: The behavior of Series.replace (and DataFrame.replace) with CategoricalDtype is deprecated. In a future version, replace will only be used for cases that preserve the categories. To change the categories, use ser.cat.rename_categories instead.
  adata.obs["Cell Type"] = adata.obs["Cell Type"].replace(
In [15]:
coords = adata.obsm["spatial"]

plt.figure(figsize=(10,10))
plt.scatter(
    coords[:,0],
    coords[:,1],
    c=adata.obs["leiden"].astype(int),
    s=1,
    cmap="tab20"
)
plt.gca().invert_yaxis()  # optional for imaging datasets
plt.show()
In [16]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

cell_types = adata.obs["Cell Type"].unique()

for cell_type in cell_types:

    adata_sub = adata[adata.obs["Cell Type"] == cell_type]
    coords = adata_sub.obsm["spatial"]

    plt.figure(figsize=(8, 8))

    plt.scatter(
        coords[:, 0],
        coords[:, 1],
        c=adata_sub.obs["leiden"].astype(int),
        s=1,
        cmap="Blues"
    )

    plt.gca().invert_yaxis()
    plt.title(cell_type)
    plt.axis("off")
    plt.show()
In [19]:
sc.tl.leiden(
    adata,
    resolution=0.5,
    flavor="igraph",
    n_iterations=2,
    directed=False
)

sc.pl.umap(
    adata,
    color="leiden"
)
In [20]:
adata.write("Mouse_S1R3.h5ad")
from google.colab import files
files.download("Mouse_S1R3.h5ad")
